roles.title: Chief Executive Officer
roles.purposeOfRole: set the company's strategic direction, steward the brand's identity and long-term value, and ensure Vosges Haut-Chocolat grows profitably while remaining true to its founding vision of chocolate as cultural and emotional experience.
roles.jobDescription: Define and hold the multi-year strategic roadmap. Co-own financial stewardship with the COO — P&L, capital structure, and investor/owner relationships. Build and lead the executive team. Act as the primary external face of the brand. Drive revenue growth through the brand, product, and customer-facing sales channels (DTC, retail, wholesale presence). Decide when to reweight the organization's focus across growth, operations, and brand.
roles.note: CEO scorecard focuses on STRATEGIC leadership, not day-to-day operations.

initiatives.name: Own the 3-year strategic plan
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold and evolve the multi-year roadmap that ties brand, portfolio, channel mix, and margin profile together. Co-own the plan with the COO as peers; bring the CEO lens on brand, portfolio, and customer-facing growth. Partner with the Board, LT, and ownership on annual priorities and capital allocation.
initiatives.successMetric: Strategic plan documented, dated, and reviewed with LT quarterly. Annual priorities traceable to the plan. 90% of executive decisions reference the plan in writing. CEO-specific lens on the plan = brand, portfolio, and customer-facing channels.

initiatives.name: Own financial stewardship and owner alignment
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold enterprise P&L ownership jointly with the COO. Bring the CEO lens — brand revenue mechanisms, product margin, channel mix — to forecasting, capital allocation, and scenario modeling. Partner with Finance on the monthly close. Keep ownership fully informed.
initiatives.successMetric: Annual budget on time with scenario ranges. Monthly close reviewed with ownership within 15 business days. Gross margin at or above plan. CEO-specific metrics: brand revenue growth, DTC + retail + wholesale channel mix, product margin.

initiatives.name: Represent Vosges externally
initiatives.desiredResult: Be the face of the brand with trade partners, press, key accounts, and the luxury and food community. Open doors the rest of the company can't. Partner with the COO when external relationships cross into manufacturing, private label, or co-packing.
initiatives.successMetric: ≥12 strategic relationships actively cultivated in 2026. Each external commitment tied to a written expected outcome within 30 days. Zero public-facing miscommunications.

initiatives.name: Hold leaders accountable to scorecards and OKRs
initiatives.desiredResult: Run the scorecard and OKR framework as a management system for the executive team. Review each direct report's scorecard quarterly. Hire, calibrate, coach, and hold the standard for the top layer of the organization. Build and retain A-player executives.
initiatives.successMetric: Every direct report has an active scorecard and quarterly coaching review. Off-track items get a dated recovery plan within 2 weeks. Executive seats filled with A-players within 90 days of vacancy. No direct report requires more than 2 hours/week of CEO escalation.

initiatives.name: Drive brand-led revenue growth
initiatives.desiredResult: Own revenue growth through the Vosges brand, product portfolio, and customer-facing sales channels — DTC, retail boutiques, wholesale brand presence. Protect what Vosges means in the market while expanding commercial reach. Sponsor portfolio rationalization, channel expansion, and the product innovation roadmap.
initiatives.successMetric: Brand revenue at or above plan by channel. Zero launches that cannot articulate a brand rationale. Portfolio rationalization decisions traceable to CEO review. DTC, retail, and brand-led wholesale metrics owned end-to-end.

skills: Brand instinct paired with commercial discipline
Financial fluency — P&L, capital structure, scenario modeling
Restraint — governs the system rather than overriding it

roles.title: Chief Operating Officer
roles.purposeOfRole: operationalize enterprise strategy — make sure the company delivers on what it promises customers, keeps unit economics healthy, and holds every operational leader accountable to a shared set of plans, numbers, and cadences.
roles.jobDescription: Own execution across Operations, Supply Chain, Quality, Customer Service, and Technology. Co-own financial stewardship with the CEO as peers. Drive revenue growth through the company's manufacturing and production capabilities — private label, co-packer relationships, and B2B manufacturing capacity. Hold operating leaders to their scorecards. Protect margin and reliability while the company grows.
roles.note: COO scorecard focuses on STRATEGIC operating leadership — building the operating system, not doing the work.

initiatives.name: Own the 3-year strategic plan
initiatives.desiredResult: Co-own the multi-year roadmap with the CEO as peers; bring the COO lens on operations, manufacturing capacity, supply chain, and production economics. Make sure every strategic commitment has an operations and capacity answer behind it.
initiatives.successMetric: Strategic plan documented, dated, and reviewed with LT quarterly. 90% of executive decisions reference the plan in writing. COO-specific lens = manufacturing capacity, supply chain readiness, production economics, and B2B/co-manufacturing channel enablement.

initiatives.name: Own financial stewardship and owner alignment
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold enterprise P&L ownership jointly with the CEO. Bring the COO lens — production efficiency, lean manufacturing practices, COGS, fulfillment cost — to forecasting and capital allocation. Partner with Finance on the monthly close. Protect enterprise gross margin through costing accuracy, purchase discipline, and yield improvement.
initiatives.successMetric: Annual budget on time with scenario ranges. Monthly close reviewed with ownership within 15 business days. Gross margin at or above plan by channel. COO-specific metrics: production efficiency, lean manufacturing gains, COGS variance, fulfillment cost per order. Zero major margin surprises after month-close.

initiatives.name: Represent Vosges externally
initiatives.desiredResult: Be the company's external face for manufacturing, private label, co-packer, and B2B relationships. Partner with the CEO on relationships that cross brand, retail, and trade. Represent Vosges in industry, regulatory, and capability-partner forums.
initiatives.successMetric: ≥8 strategic manufacturing/private-label/co-packer relationships actively cultivated in 2026. Each external commitment tied to a written expected outcome within 30 days. Zero public-facing miscommunications.

initiatives.name: Hold operating leaders accountable to scorecards and OKRs
initiatives.desiredResult: Run the scorecard and OKR framework as a management system for the operating leadership — Director of Operations, Head of Technology, and the full operating bench. Review each direct report's scorecard quarterly. Own the end-to-end customer fulfillment promise — lead times, ship dates, quality, service recovery. Sponsor the 3–5 capability build-outs the next chapter requires (B2B infrastructure, forecasting, BOMs 2.0, data integration, SQF).
initiatives.successMetric: Every direct report has an active scorecard and quarterly coaching review. Off-track items get a dated recovery plan within 2 weeks. OTIF at or above target by channel every month. Customer-facing failures tracked and trending down QoQ. Capability portfolio published and reviewed quarterly; each project has owner, dated outcome, and stop-or-finish gate.

initiatives.name: Drive manufacturing-led revenue growth
initiatives.desiredResult: Own revenue growth through Vosges' manufacturing and production capabilities — private label, co-packer partnerships, B2B manufacturing capacity. Monetize the factory when it isn't making Vosges-branded product. Partner with the Wholesale & Private Label Sales seat on opportunity cycles. Protect Vosges-brand capacity commitments while growing manufacturing revenue.
initiatives.successMetric: Private-label + co-packer + manufacturing-capacity revenue at or above plan. Capacity utilization at or above target without harming branded OTIF. Every private-label commitment reviewed by COO before sign. Manufacturing-channel pipeline reviewed quarterly with ownership.

skills: Operating-system thinking — building cadence and holding it
Cross-functional leadership across Ops, Tech, and Customer
Ability to hold complexity without collapsing into doing the work
Strategic patience — installing systems that outlast any single leader

Run the marketing calendar through Marketing, Performance Marketing, and Art Direction. Run Shopify Plus DTC through the Ecom Manager. Hit revenue while protecting the brand experience.

initiatives.name: Hit the DTC revenue plan
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the DTC number. Build and run the merchandising and promotional calendar. Balance acquisition investment against retention. Report weekly against plan.
initiatives.successMetric: DTC revenue at or above plan by quarter. Conversion rate trending up YoY. AOV at or above plan. Monthly variance analysis documented and reviewed.

initiatives.name: Hold the 12–18 month product roadmap
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with the Senior Product Manager on a published roadmap that reflects strategic priorities (holiday, grab-and-go, wholesale, B2B). Update quarterly; balance portfolio load against team capacity.
initiatives.successMetric: Roadmap reviewed with LT quarterly. Every active concept has a named Stage-Gate status. Portfolio load balanced against capacity with no stuck concepts.

initiatives.name: Steward brand voice end-to-end
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Art Director, Marketing Manager, and CEO on brand-voice consistency across every touchpoint — site, email, packaging, paid, PR. Catch drift early.
initiatives.successMetric: Brand voice guidelines documented and referenced in every creative brief. Quarterly brand audit across channels. Zero publicly-documented off-brand incidents in 2026.

initiatives.name: Land the 2026 launch calendar on time, on brand, on margin
initiatives.desiredResult: Drive Holiday 2026, grab-and-go, and seasonal launches through the org. Own cross-functional readiness — Marketing, Sales, Ops, Fulfillment — for every launch.
initiatives.successMetric: Holiday 2026 shipped on schedule with ≥95% of SKUs hitting planned launch date. Post-launch readiness score ≥4/5 from functional partners across every launch.

initiatives.name: Run peak-season without operational failure
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Operations, NPD, and Customer Service on peak-period readiness. Pre-plan capacity, communicate cutoffs early, hold the line when the calendar says hold.
initiatives.successMetric: Holiday and Mother's Day peaks run within plan. OTIF ≥ target through peak. Zero high-profile CX failures. Post-peak debrief documented with learnings captured.

skills: DTC e-commerce leadership on Shopify Plus
Brand + product + commercial fluency in a single seat
Cross-functional leadership across creative, commercial, and operations
Data literacy — reads Shopify + GA + retention cohorts

roles.title: Director of Sales
roles.location: Chicago (on-site initially, hybrid eligible after onboarding)
roles.purposeOfRole: lead the B2B Sales organization — ensuring salespeople have the tools, processes, and support they need to capture and protect revenue, with end-to-end accountability from sale capture through fulfillment.
roles.jobDescription: Build and lead the B2B Sales organization, starting with Corporate Gifting and extending into Wholesale and Private Label. Partner with Director of Operations on SLAs and co-lead weekly Sales & Ops meetings. Reconfigure HubSpot for B2B pipeline management and win/loss tracking. Develop standardized product offerings and sales materials. Keep salespeople focused on selling while the organization delivers on every commitment.
roles.note: Andy's gold-standard scorecard — the structural reference for every other role.

initiatives.name: Establish and lead the B2B Sales organization structure
initiatives.desiredResult: Build the foundation — Sales Ops support, defined roles (Sales Account Manager, Junior AM, Wholesale & Private Label), team cadences, and communication protocols. Keep salespeople focused on selling.
initiatives.successMetric: B2B Sales org fully operational with clear accountability. Salespeople focused exclusively on selling and relationships, not troubleshooting fulfillment.

initiatives.name: Partner with Director of Operations on SLAs
initiatives.desiredResult: Define mutual commitments between Sales and Operations — lead times, cutoffs, escalation protocols, capacity constraints. Co-lead the weekly Sales & Ops meeting.
initiatives.successMetric: Documented SLAs in place and honored by both sides. Weekly Sales & Ops meeting running with structured agenda. Zero surprise fulfillment failures from miscommunication.

initiatives.name: Reconfigure HubSpot for B2B
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Head of Technology to rebuild HubSpot with proper pipelines, stages, required fields, and win/loss tracking that gives leadership a real-time view of revenue at risk.
initiatives.successMetric: B2B pipeline live with win/loss tracking. Weekly reporting on pipeline health, conversion, and revenue at risk. Loss reasons captured on 100% of closed-lost deals.

initiatives.name: Establish standardized B2B offering and sales materials
initiatives.desiredResult: No standardized B2B offering exists today. Establish it in 2026 — approved configurations, customization rules, lead times, MOQs, and pricing. Create collateral that enables confident selling within approved bounds. Partner with Senior Product Manager on what's buildable and with Operations on what's fulfillable.
initiatives.successMetric: Published B2B catalog/menu live in 2026. Salespeople can confidently commit to what we can fulfill. Ad-hoc customization requests escalated through the Director of Sales, not improvised. Zero fulfillment-risk configurations shipped.

initiatives.name: Protect B2B revenue through risk management
initiatives.desiredResult: Build the visibility and cadence that flags at-risk orders before the customer feels it. Real-time revenue-at-risk view for leadership.
initiatives.successMetric: Fulfillment-driven sales losses down 50% vs H2 2025. Risks flagged ≥48 hours ahead of customer impact. Zero escalations requiring CEO intervention.

skills: B2B sales leadership with cross-functional influence
Balances client advocacy with operational realities

Lead the airport strategy — turning every O'Hare traveler into a future Vosges e-commerce customer. Own climate control, food safety, and stock rotation at retail. Coordinate with Oakley leadership on the transition plan (Oakley closure is likely).

initiatives.name: Hit retail revenue + margin plan
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the multi-unit P&L across Oakley and the two O'Hare boutiques. Manage labor cost. Hit contribution margin by unit. Report weekly against plan with variance commentary. Honor the Oakley transition plan as it unfolds.
initiatives.successMetric: Retail revenue at or above plan by unit. Labor-as-percent-of-revenue within target. Contribution margin at or above plan by unit. Weekly variance readout delivered on time.

initiatives.name: Grow foot traffic and transaction value at O'Hare
initiatives.desiredResult: Make the O'Hare boutiques the company's highest-performing retail square footage. Partner with Ivana on visual merchandising, assortment, and the airport-appropriate experience (food-handling regs prevent open sampling; build the theatre a different way). Optimize for impulse and gifting.
initiatives.successMetric: O'Hare foot traffic up YoY. Transaction value up YoY per boutique. Assortment reviewed quarterly with sell-through data. Weekly readout on both airport units delivered on time.

initiatives.name: Use airport boutiques as brand amplifiers for e-com
initiatives.desiredResult: Convert airport customers — non-Chicago travelers — into recurring Vosges e-commerce customers. Build the in-boutique mechanics (QR, receipt-to-cart, in-bag inserts, post-purchase flows) that make one airport purchase trigger at least one subsequent e-com order. Partner with Ecom Manager and Performance Marketing on the measurement loop.
initiatives.successMetric: Target: every O'Hare customer places at least one e-commerce order after their boutique visit. Airport-attributed e-com order rate measured monthly and trending up QoQ. Conversion loop documented and reviewed with Ecom and Performance Marketing quarterly.

initiatives.name: Build the retail leadership pipeline
initiatives.desiredResult: Develop Ivana and boutique leads into stronger managers. Name successors per unit. Make careers at Vosges real for the retail bench — especially as the O'Hare footprint becomes the strategic center.
initiatives.successMetric: Every boutique has a named, ready-in-6-months successor. Internal-fill rate on boutique manager openings ≥70%. Development plans active for Ivana and all boutique leads.

initiatives.name: Run retail food safety without compromise
initiatives.desiredResult: FIFO, climate, sanitation at every unit. Train, audit, and enforce — with airport-specific requirements baked in.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero climate-related product losses. Zero customer-facing food safety incidents. Audit score ≥ standard every quarter across all three boutiques.

initiatives.successMetric: Ship-on-time ≥98% daily. Backlog trending to zero by end of shift. Peak throughput within plan.

initiatives.name: Own fulfillment accuracy
initiatives.desiredResult: Enforce pick and pack standards. Audit variance weekly. Close gaps with training and tooling.
initiatives.successMetric: Fulfillment accuracy ≥99%. Weekly variance audit documented. Root-cause actions closed within 14 days.

initiatives.name: Develop the pack-line bench
initiatives.desiredResult: Cross-train the team. Named backups for every station.
initiatives.successMetric: Every station ≥2 deep on trained staff. Cross-training matrix current. Zero single-point-of-failure stations.

initiatives.name: Manage peak capacity
initiatives.desiredResult: Staff peaks in advance. Coordinate with the Ops Manager on flex labor and overtime.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero peak-period backlog days. Flex labor plan in place 30 days before peak. Overtime within plan.

initiatives.name: Partner with Customer Tech Ops on ShipStation and order tech
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Customer Tech Operations on ShipStation and other order-related issues related to the technology systems. Escalate integration issues. Trend down manual touches on orders through tech, not through headcount.
initiatives.successMetric: Manual ship touches down quarter-over-quarter. Zero ShipStation outages >2 hours without escalation. Joint Fulfillment–Customer-Tech-Ops review held monthly.

roles.title: Procurement Manager
roles.purposeOfRole: own end-to-end sourcing, purchasing, and inventory management — making sure Vosges has the ingredients and packaging it needs at the right cost, quality, and timing, without tying up cash in excess inventory.
roles.jobDescription: Lead sourcing and purchasing for all ingredients, packaging, and indirect materials. Manage vendor relationships. Own inventory levels, reorder points, and safety stock. Partner with NPD on sourcing for new products. Partner with Finance on cost and cash. Drive Fishbowl as the source of truth. Coach the Inventory Specialist.
roles.note: Title changed from 'Supply Chain Manager' to 'Procurement Manager'. Per 04-18 review: must include 'no out-of-stocks on everyday items' initiative. Owns stock-level OKRs for Dir Ops.

initiatives.name: No out-of-stocks on everyday items
initiatives.desiredResult: Prioritized 2026 initiative, migrated down from the COO/Director of Ops OKR. Own the execution of the everyday-SKU stock-level KR. Director of Operations is accountable for the overall OKR; Procurement Manager does the majority of the work — reorder discipline, vendor responsiveness, inbound timing, and exception handling on the everyday-SKU list.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero stock-outs on the everyday-SKU list in 2026. Weekly stock-level readout delivered. Every exception logged, root-caused, and closed with a dated action. Director of Ops sign-off that Procurement is executing the OKR share.

initiatives.name: Build vendor and sourcing strategy
initiatives.desiredResult: Segment vendors by criticality. Qualify dual sources on Class-A ingredients and packaging.
initiatives.successMetric: 2+ qualified sources for every Class-A item. Vendor segmentation documented and reviewed annually.

initiatives.name: Own inventory discipline
initiatives.desiredResult: Hold reorder points, safety stock, and turns targets tuned to the forecast. Prevent the everyday-SKU OOS condition through reorder discipline.
initiatives.successMetric: Inventory turns +1 on Class-A vs 2025 baseline. Safety-stock days within target. Dead stock trending down.

initiatives.name: Run Fishbowl as the procurement source of truth
initiatives.desiredResult: 100% of POs in Fishbowl. Reconcile monthly with Flavor Studio.
initiatives.successMetric: Drift ≤2% on costcodes and pricing monthly. Zero POs outside Fishbowl. Monthly reconciliation clean.

initiatives.name: Protect holiday, launch, and Stage-Gate lead times
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the procurement calendar. Run 90-day risk reviews per named launch. Attach a sourcing feasibility assessment to every concept before Stage-Gate commit (absorbed from the prior Stage-Gate sourcing initiative).
initiatives.successMetric: Zero launch stock-outs attributable to procurement. 90-day risk review on file for every named launch. Sourcing assessment on 100% of Stage-2+ concepts.

skills: Vendor management across food ingredients and packaging
Cross-functional partnership with NPD, Finance, Ops

roles.title: Inventory Specialist
roles.purposeOfRole: own the data discipline of Vosges inventory — cycle counts, receiving accuracy, Fishbowl integrity, and reorder-point maintenance — so the business runs on a number everyone can trust.
roles.jobDescription: Execute cycle counts. Reconcile Fishbowl against physical inventory. Own receiving accuracy. Maintain reorder points and min/max levels. Flag inventory risks to the Procurement Manager. Partner with Product Operations on BOM data integrity.
roles.note: Reports to Procurement Manager. In Current state, was 'Fulfillment Supervisor' under Supply Chain — role refocused.

initiatives.name: Maintain Fishbowl inventory accuracy
initiatives.desiredResult: Run daily cycle counts on assigned zones. Reconcile variances within 48 hours.
initiatives.successMetric: Inventory accuracy ≥98% monthly. Cycle-count cadence held. Variances resolved within 48 hours ≥95% of the time.

initiatives.name: Own purchase-order flow
initiatives.desiredResult: PO entry, receiving reconciliation, vendor follow-up on open POs.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero orphan POs >30 days. PO-to-receipt match rate ≥98%. Vendor follow-up logged.

initiatives.name: Manage safety stock and reorder triggers
initiatives.desiredResult: Monitor reorder points daily. Flag gaps before they become stock-outs.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero Class-A stock-outs. Reorder triggers reviewed weekly. Flags escalated to Procurement Manager with 2 weeks' lead.

initiatives.name: Run monthly inventory reporting
initiatives.desiredResult: Closing inventory, aging, turns, and slow-movers. Deliver to the Procurement Manager and Finance on schedule.
initiatives.successMetric: Monthly inventory report delivered on time. Aging-stock trends documented. Slow-mover list actioned monthly.

initiatives.name: Support cycle count and physical inventory
initiatives.desiredResult: Lead the cycle-count program. Own the execution of the annual physical count.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero cycle-count schedule misses. Annual physical count completed with variance within standard.

skills: Cross-functional partnership with Product Operations and Ops
Comfortable on the floor and in the data

roles.title: QA Manager
roles.purposeOfRole: protect Vosges customers, team members, and brand by owning food safety, quality assurance, and regulatory compliance across the organization.
roles.jobDescription: Own food safety (SQF, HACCP, GMP), quality assurance programs, and regulatory compliance (FDA, Canadian/Mexican export, allergen controls). Lead audits. Be the final authority on product holds, releases, and CAPAs. Partner with Ops, Procurement, and NPD so quality is built in, not inspected in.
roles.note: KEY STRUCTURAL MOVE: Moves from COO to Director of Operations. Consolidated QA leadership.

initiatives.name: Pass SQF with zero major NCs
initiatives.desiredResult: Mock audits, gap closure, training, and audit execution. Own the full SQF readiness program.
initiatives.successMetric: SQF passed on schedule in 2026 with zero major non-conformances. Mock audits completed on plan.

initiatives.name: Build the floor QA coverage model
initiatives.desiredResult: Assess gaps with the Ops Manager. Staff and document the new coverage model.
initiatives.successMetric: Spec-drift incidents trending down monthly. Coverage model documented and staffed to plan.

initiatives.name: Own regulatory and labeling compliance
initiatives.desiredResult: FDA, Canadian, and Mexican requirements across every active SKU. Lead labeling audits before launch.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero regulatory findings or recalls. Labeling audit completed on 100% of launches pre-ship.

initiatives.name: Drive the Blame-the-Process-First culture
initiatives.desiredResult: Root-cause analysis on every quality incident. Publish learnings. Teach supervisors to see the process, not the person.
initiatives.successMetric: Repeat-cause incidents down quarterly. Root-cause published on 100% of incidents. Supervisor RCA certification complete by Q3.

initiatives.name: Partner with NPD on design-for-quality
initiatives.desiredResult: Embed QA gates at Stage 3 and Stage 4. Make specs manufacturable before launch.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero post-launch quality reformulations. QA sign-off on 100% of Stage 3 and Stage 4 gate reviews.

skills: Cross-functional influence with Ops and NPD

roles.title: Customer Service Manager
roles.purposeOfRole: lead the Chocolate Concierge Department so every Vosges customer — DTC, retail, and B2B follow-up — receives responsive, warm, and accurate service that reinforces the brand promise.
roles.jobDescription: Manage and mentor the service team. Own CSAT, NPS, response-time, and contact-deflection KPIs. Build workflows and playbooks. Handle escalations. Channel customer voice back into Product and Commerce so the same issues stop reaching the customer.
roles.note: KEY STRUCTURAL MOVE: Moves from COO to Director of Operations. Partners closely with Customer Tech Operations.

initiatives.name: Hit CSAT, NPS, and response-time targets
initiatives.desiredResult: Own the top service KPIs. Build standards. Audit weekly.
initiatives.successMetric: First-response SLA met ≥95% of the time. CSAT at or above target. NPS trending up QoQ.

initiatives.name: Build the service playbook and training
initiatives.desiredResult: Document recurring scenarios. Certify agents through a standard onboarding program.
initiatives.successMetric: New-hire ramp time down 30% vs 2025. 100% of agents certified on the playbook. Playbook updated quarterly.

initiatives.name: Partner with Customer Tech Ops on Gorgias
initiatives.desiredResult: Co-own macros, rules, routing, and integrations. Drive resolution time through tooling rather than headcount.
initiatives.successMetric: Manual triage time down 25%. Resolution time improving QoQ. Tooling health reviewed quarterly.

initiatives.name: Channel customer voice into the business
initiatives.desiredResult: Monthly VOC readout to Ecom, NPD, Ops, and Retail. Turn complaints into systemic changes.
initiatives.successMetric: ≥3 systemic changes per quarter traceable to VOC. Monthly readout delivered on schedule.

initiatives.name: Handle escalations and protect the brand
initiatives.desiredResult: Own high-priority escalations personally. Make the customer whole or escalate internally with a dated plan.
initiatives.successMetric: 100% of escalations resolved within SLA. Zero public escalations requiring CEO intervention.

roles.title: Head of Technology
roles.purposeOfRole: own the technology backbone of Vosges — Shopify, Fishbowl, Flavor Studio, HubSpot, data integrations, infrastructure, and IT — making sure the right systems exist, work together, and evolve with the business.
roles.jobDescription: Own technology strategy, architecture, vendor management, and data integration. Maintain and evolve the core stack (Shopify Plus, Fishbowl, Flavor Studio, HubSpot). Own security, access, and IT operations. Coach the Customer Tech Operations seat. Partner with every functional leader on tech-enabled improvement.
roles.note: IT box on the org chart = this seat (per Andy). Connective service under COO.

initiatives.name: Publish the tech roadmap and architecture
initiatives.desiredResult: Document the current-state architecture. Build a 12-month roadmap tied to business outcomes, not projects.
initiatives.successMetric: Architecture and roadmap published and reviewed with LT quarterly. Every roadmap item tied to a business outcome.

initiatives.name: Make Fishbowl the operational source of truth
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with Procurement, Product Operations, and Finance. Close down parallel systems and spreadsheets.
initiatives.successMetric: System drift ≤2% monthly. Zero critical parallel systems by Q3. Monthly reconciliation clean.

initiatives.name: Own integrations and stack data quality
initiatives.desiredResult: Shopify, HubSpot, Gorgias, Flavor Studio, Fishbowl healthy end-to-end. Monitor and respond within SLA.
initiatives.successMetric: Integration incidents resolved within SLA ≥95% of the time. Zero data-quality incidents >24 hours.

initiatives.name: Own security, access, and IT basics
initiatives.desiredResult: MFA, access reviews, device management, backup, and incident response. No theater — real hygiene.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero unauthorized-access incidents. Quarterly access review completed. Backup restore tested quarterly.

initiatives.name: Partner on tech-enabled improvement
initiatives.desiredResult: Help Sales, NPD, Ops, and CS pick and use the right tools. Sponsor the 3–5 highest-impact improvements.
initiatives.successMetric: ≥4 major tech-enabled improvements delivered in 2026 with documented outcomes.

skills: Technology leadership in a multi-system CPG / retail environment
Business-first orientation — leads with outcome, not tool

roles.title: Customer Tech Operations
roles.purposeOfRole: own the successful implementation, maintenance, and continuous improvement of the customer-facing technology stack — Gorgias (support), HubSpot (sales CRM and B2B marketing; NOT service), Shopify customer-facing integrations, Zest, ShipperHQ, and the order stack — so the customer experience is reliable and the Sales and Support teams can act on clean data. Also: back up the Ecom Manager on sales-side systems.
roles.jobDescription: Own the customer-facing technology stack. Gorgias for support. HubSpot configured as the sales CRM and B2B marketing engine (NOT as a service tool — Vosges does not use HubSpot for service). Partner with Customer Service and the Sales Director. Support the Ecom Manager on Zest, the B2B Shopify instance, and the retail Shopify instances — and provide backup coverage when the Ecom Manager is unavailable. Configure, maintain, and improve each tool. Own integrations and data flow.
roles.note: ⚠️ SCORECARD UNDER REVIEW (Andy flagged 2026-04-18). Uses Head of Technology scorecard via inheritance. Per 04-18 review: HubSpot is for sales CRM/B2B marketing, NOT service. Initiatives split: (1) Gorgias/WISMO reduction, (2) HubSpot strategic capabilities with Sales Director.

initiatives.name: Stabilize the customer-facing tech stack
initiatives.desiredResult: Audit every tool. Close gaps. Document owners and escalation paths. Broader 2026 framing — replaces the prior 'Fix ShipperHQ / Quick Pay / calendar' project scope, which was too narrow.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero CX failures from misconfiguration by Q3. Tool inventory current. Escalation paths documented. Peak-period lock-down held without regression.

initiatives.name: Reduce WISMO tickets and generate revenue via Gorgias
initiatives.desiredResult: Own reducing 'Where Is My Order' tickets through Gorgias automation, AI-assisted response, and upstream integration. Turn Gorgias from a cost-center tool into a revenue-generation touchpoint (targeted offers, proactive recovery, cross-sell in support flows).
initiatives.successMetric: WISMO ticket volume down ≥30% YoY. Gorgias-attributed revenue tracked and trending up. AI-assisted resolution deployed on ≥50% of qualifying ticket types. Quarterly Gorgias health check documented.

initiatives.name: Enable HubSpot for Sales — lead capture, prospecting, deal closure
initiatives.desiredResult: Partner with the Director of Sales to enable strategic capabilities within HubSpot as the sales CRM and B2B marketing engine. Lead capture flows, prospecting workflows, deal-stage pipelines, and closure support. Retire any lingering 'HubSpot = service' configuration — Vosges does not use HubSpot for service.
initiatives.successMetric: B2B pipeline live with win/loss tracking in HubSpot. Lead-capture flows active on the site and tracked to deal. Sales team reports HubSpot is usable daily. Zero service routing still configured in HubSpot by Q2.

initiatives.name: Own stack data quality
initiatives.desiredResult: Accurate customer and order flow across Shopify, HubSpot, Fishbowl, and Gorgias. Monthly reconciliation with the operating owners of each tool.
initiatives.successMetric: Zero CX failures from data drift. Monthly data-quality report delivered. Drift resolved within 7 days. Joint review with Ecom Manager and Customer Service Manager quarterly.

initiatives.name: Back up the Ecom Manager on sales-side systems
initiatives.desiredResult: Serve as the support-and-backup seat for the Ecom Manager on Zest, the B2B Shopify instance, and the retail Shopify instances. Be trained enough to cover the Ecom Manager during absences and to unblock issues in real time. Partner daily on the sales/support stack boundary.
initiatives.successMetric: Backup coverage documented. Ecom Manager absence covered without CX impact at least 2x in 2026. Zest accuracy ≥99% post-launch. B2B Shopify stable through first 30 days. Joint sales/support stack review held monthly.
